Site Rules & FAQs

Everything you need to know about joining our community

Site Rules

  • Please be nice — we're a friendly community!
  • No offensive content or behaviour
  • No spreading of any copyrighted material
  • Admin decisions are final
  • No spamming or unauthorised advertising

Frequently Asked Questions

How long is your course?

There is ongoing learning from one term to the next. However, if you wish to just do a 'course' we can arrange this too. Our regular classes run throughout the year, and students progress at their own pace.

What sort of equipment do I need to get started?

You will need a lace pillow, bobbins, pins, thread, card and some other items which can usually be found around the house. Don't worry about buying everything at once — we can advise you on what to get and when.

Where do I get this equipment?

Our tutor Amanda can provide some equipment for you to borrow until you have decided that you enjoy the craft and wish to buy your own. There are some items of equipment which you will need to buy, and we can advise on this. See our Lace Suppliers page for recommended suppliers.

How long does it take to learn the basic stitches?

Not very long really! It is a bit like knitting — there are 2 stitches, cloth stitch and half stitch, plus a twist. EVERYTHING else is made up of these 2 stitches. Most students learn these 2 stitches in the first 2 weeks of the term.

Do I need any prior experience?

Not at all! We welcome complete beginners. Our classes are designed to take you from absolute novice to confident lacemaker. We also welcome experienced lacemakers who want to join our community.

How much do classes cost?

Please contact us for current pricing. We aim to keep our classes affordable and accessible to everyone.

Can I try a class before committing?

Absolutely! We encourage prospective students to come along and try a session. It's the best way to see if bobbin lace is for you and to meet our friendly group.

Still Have Questions?

Don't see your question here? Get in touch and we'll be happy to help.

Contact Us Email Direct
